name: Carriage 1 — The Engine
summary: The forward carriage. There is no driver. There has never needed to be one. The Train is the thing that drives, and you have come up to its head.

notable_features: The instrument panel is brass and bakelite, ticking in three places. The dials are not labelled. They are reading something, but the units belong to whatever decides where the Train goes next.
Where the driver's seat should be, there is a hollow in the floor, worn the shape of a man kneeling. The wood is dark with use. Nothing has knelt here in your lifetime, and yet something has.

A single brass lever rises from the panel at waist height. Two positions, etched in capitals: STOP and CONTINUE. The lever is warm under the hand. The metal remembers what you are about to do, the way a key remembers a lock.

Mounted on the forward bulkhead is a mirror the size of a window. Your reflection in it is yours. Your reflection is not doing what you are doing. The lag is not the lag of a slow mirror. It is the lag of someone waiting their turn.

On a small lectern beside the lever is a passenger manifest, open. One name is underlined. The name is not in the language you have been speaking, but you can read it. You have been able to read it for some time.

san_triggers.trigger_text: The floor has been worn the shape of a kneeling man. The shape fits you.
Your reflection is waiting. You have been the reflection for some of this.
Your name is on the manifest, underlined, in a script you have been able to read longer than you should have been able to.
The lever is warm. It is warm because you have already touched it, in a turn that has not happened.

skill_check_hooks.action_hint: read the underlined name on the manifest

puzzle.description: The final choice. The lever moves either way. What it means depends on what you have already understood. The Train does not insist on a particular answer; it only insists that one is given.
puzzle.unlock_condition: Player makes the final choice at the lever. The ending taken is computed from fragments_heard, the underlined-name reading (Library Use regular), and whether the mirror was met (Psychology extreme).
